source: League Start Wealth Plan: A Guide for Profit Crafting & Investing (3.29 Update), cross-checked against the official 3.29 patch notes and the July 23 strategy/corruption video review
guardrails: Crafting is passive income, not a replacement for atlas progression or playing the game.
Verify the live input cost, realistic sale price, and sale depth before committing currency.
Keep a liquid reserve; never lock the entire bankroll into crafts or appreciating assets.
Set the acceptable outcome and stop-loss before the first craft or flip.
Prefer several sellable good items over one perfect item during the first week.
Every listing, whisper, purchase, sale, vendor trade, and craft is performed manually.
Plan the atlas strategy and build together: clear speed, damage projection, boss damage, recovery, and movement speed determine which market inputs you can produce efficiently.

stages.goal: Rush atlas completion while building a broad inventory of affordable life, resistance, movement-speed, and build-enabling items.
Rotate out of generic rares and into meta-targeted crafts, cluster jewels, and repeatable low-capital arbitrage.
Scale proven crafts, bulk flips, and appreciating positions without sacrificing liquidity.
Concentrate only in proven high-margin crafts, scarce assets, and bulk markets with demonstrated sale depth.

strategies.name: Identify and list useful rare drops
strategies.action: Identify and price life, resistance, attribute, movement-speed, and open-affix rares instead of vendoring everything.
strategies.verify: Compare against realistic similar listings and price to sell, not against a single optimistic listing.
strategies.stop_loss: Vendor or reprice stale generic rares as the market moves past them.

strategies.name: Build early inventory with Rog
strategies.action: Use Rog to produce many decent armour pieces and life/resistance jewellery; accept good outcomes rather than chasing perfection.
strategies.verify: Prioritize weak or high-demand slots and confirm the resulting item has a realistic buyer.
strategies.stop_loss: Stop upgrading once the item is broadly sellable; do not turn a good item into a lottery ticket.

strategies.name: Essence life/resistance gear
strategies.action: Use screaming or better essences on appropriate bases to make boots, armour, belts, and jewellery with life plus resistances.
strategies.verify: Check base level, open affixes, essence price, and the current price of a merely good result.
strategies.stop_loss: Aim for a sellable combination, then list it; do not spend premium essences forcing perfect suffixes.

strategies.name: Meta cluster-jewel inventory
strategies.action: Craft cluster bases required by rising ladder builds, selling acceptable two- and three-notable outcomes instead of one exact jewel only.
strategies.verify: Confirm the base type, item level, passive count, desired notables, input cost, and recent sale depth.
strategies.stop_loss: Pivot or sell partial successes when the meta or fossil/Harvest cost changes.

strategies.name: Level high-demand gems in weapon swap
strategies.action: Fill spare weapon-swap sockets with currently valuable gems, then sell leveled gems or manually corrupt selected ones.
strategies.verify: Use current gem prices and avoid gems whose quality cost erases the margin.
strategies.stop_loss: Rotate gems when their leveled premium falls below the opportunity cost of the sockets.

strategies.name: Vendor-recipe arbitrage
strategies.action: Compare component costs with outputs such as The Taming, Kingmaker, The Anima Stone, Combat Focus jewels, or Lost Unity before assembling manually.
strategies.verify: Require a margin after all components, travel time, slow buys, and realistic output sale price.
strategies.stop_loss: Do not buy components until the entire recipe is profitable and the output has sale depth.

strategies.name: Cheap-scarab three-to-one vendoring
strategies.action: Slow-buy very cheap scarabs and manually vendor three of a kind for a randomized scarab when the pool has enough valuable outcomes.
strategies.verify: Estimate expected value from the current scarab pool and haircut heavily for variance and acquisition time.
strategies.stop_loss: Use a fixed batch budget; stop when the realized return or acquisition rate misses the target.

strategies.name: Harvest commodity rerolls
strategies.action: Reroll catalysts, essences, fossils, or delirium orbs only when the live ratio between cheap inputs, lifeforce, and valuable outputs is favorable.
strategies.verify: Double-check third-party calculators against live prices and include lifeforce plus failed-output liquidation.
strategies.stop_loss: Set a batch size and stop when the observed output mix falls below the required margin.

strategies.name: Tattoo or runegraft vendoring
strategies.action: Slow-buy low-cost tattoos of the same tribe or cheap runegrafts and manually vendor three-to-one when valuable outcomes support the expected value.
strategies.verify: Tribe matters for tattoos; calculate the current output pool and discount for slow acquisition.
strategies.stop_loss: Cap each batch and liquidate remaining inputs if pool values decline.

strategies.name: Six-link popular unique chests
strategies.action: Link popular unique body armours with deterministic options when available or tainted fusings when the expected margin supports the variance.
strategies.verify: Check the unlinked input, six-linked output, linking-material cost, corrupt state, socket colours, and sale depth.
strategies.stop_loss: Hold enough reserve to survive bad RNG; never fund a variance craft with the whole bankroll.

strategies.name: Craft for current ladder builds
strategies.action: Use current ladder data to identify repeated rare slots, bases, fractures, and cluster setups, then craft several versions for those builds.
strategies.verify: Confirm adoption is rising, the slot is not dominated by a unique, and comparable items actually sell.
strategies.stop_loss: Rotate inventory when build share or completed-item prices fall; do not keep crafting yesterday's meta.

strategies.name: Generate or trade double-corruption temples
strategies.action: Generate Locus of Corruption temples with Alva and sell the temple or reserve a small, separately costed share for proven corruption targets. The 3.29 corruption pool is much stronger while non-unique Synthesised bases were removed.
strategies.verify: Compare the live temple price, maps per temple, Incursion Scarab of Timelines price, and the expected value of consuming the chamber before deciding to sell or use it.
strategies.stop_loss: Default to selling the temple until a specific corruption target has enough outcome value and sale depth to beat the guaranteed temple sale.

strategies.name: 3.29 premium corruption batches
strategies.action: Run small manual batches of liquid rings, amulets, boots, shields, or caster weapons whose 3.29 corruption pool has several premium outcomes: maximum charges, aura reservation/effect, action speed, maximum resistances, gem levels, cast speed, or explode.
strategies.verify: Price the uncorrupted input, ordinary corrupted brick, each relevant implicit, double-corruption access, and real sale depth. Treat Grey Spire and Stasis Prison implicit-magnitude projects as separate extreme-risk markets.
strategies.stop_loss: Prove the market with a small fixed batch. Stop if brick liquidation or observed hit value misses the predeclared expected value; never keep corrupting to recover prior losses.

strategies.name: Medium-value bulk flipping
strategies.action: Accumulate limited-supply crafting materials such as premium chisels, conflict currency, or other frequently bulk-bought inputs and sell at a bulk premium.
strategies.verify: Require stable demand, adequate daily volume, and a spread that survives repricing and time-to-fill.
strategies.stop_loss: Do not corner thin markets; exit if the restock rate or demand invalidates the spread.

strategies.name: Staged appreciating-asset positions
strategies.action: Build partial positions in scarce, broadly demanded assets ahead of common day-3, day-7, and week-2 demand waves.
strategies.verify: Demand must be broad, supply constrained, and the thesis tied to current mechanics or builds rather than price momentum alone.
strategies.stop_loss: Scale in rather than buying at once; keep liquid currency and define both a review date and invalidation condition.

strategies.name: Influence-base reroll arbitrage
strategies.action: Create or buy influenced high-demand bases and Harvest-reroll influence toward the currently valuable influence.
strategies.verify: Check base value by influence, influence-currency cost, Harvest cost, and the chance of being left with a slow-selling base.
strategies.stop_loss: Only reroll batches whose worst acceptable influence can be liquidated without threatening the reserve.

strategies.name: High-end currency and scarce-asset flipping
strategies.action: Trade locks, mirror shards, mists, or similarly scarce assets only after the bankroll can absorb long holds and wide price moves.
strategies.verify: Use multiple real listings, recent trade direction, and realistic sale time; reject obvious price-fixing bands.
strategies.stop_loss: Size every position so a balance change or liquidity freeze cannot strand the operating bankroll.
